gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the cache ownership inversion for tiered cache behavior, alias-based legacy compatibility, root exports, star imports, and circular-import risk before merge.
scope: local disk cache reuse and eviction
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_cache.py tests/test_planner_materializer.py tests/test_engine.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q

python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/cache.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/cache.py src/document_kv_cache/__init__.py tests/test_cache.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
PYTHONPATH=src poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.pr_evidence --validate-directory pr-evidence
repository secret scan over README, package metadata, source, tests, Databricks helpers, PR evidence, and GitHub metadata
what_changed: Moved CacheTier, ChunkCacheStats, ChunkCacheResult, ByteLRU, and ChunkCache implementation into document_kv_cache.cache.
Routed document_kv_cache root cache symbols to the document-owned module.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.cache into a compatibility wrapper that aliases the document-owned cache objects.
Preserved CPU/local-disk cache behavior, stale payload protection via checksums, local-disk LRU budget eviction, and the previous legacy star-import surface.
Added regressions for document cache ownership, legacy alias compatibility, and root cache star-imports.
why: The generalized package should own tiered cache behavior because CPU and local-disk cache reuse are core to document-generic KV materialization and serving latency. Keeping legacy aliases makes the move incremental and behavior-preserving for existing restaurant imports.
